jokes.text: D'oh! [Screams]
jokes.setup: Opening credits sequence with Homer's car accident
jokes.punchline: Homer's signature exclamation of frustration
jokes.explanation: Iconic catchphrase but routine usage in opening credits

jokes.text: Hey, Dad, how come you've never taken us to see a soccer game? I don't know.
jokes.setup: Bart asks about never attending soccer
jokes.punchline: Homer's complete lack of awareness or reasoning
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer obliviousness delivered with perfect character voice

jokes.text: You'll see all your favorite soccer stars like Ariaga, Ariaga II, Bariaga, Aruglia, and Pizzoza! Oh, I never heard of those people.
jokes.setup: TV announcer lists supposed famous soccer players with ridiculous names
jokes.punchline: Homer's honest admission he doesn't know any of them
jokes.explanation: Clever satire of American unfamiliarity with soccer combined with absurd fake names

jokes.text: This match will determine once and for all which nation is the greatest on Earth - Mexico or Portugal! Can we go, Dad? Please, Dad? Yes! Yes! Oh, God, yes!
jokes.setup: Announcer oversells soccer match importance, kids beg to go
jokes.punchline: Homer's sudden desperate enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Homer's impulsive decision-making perfectly characterized

jokes.text: Oh, I'll kill myself if Portugal doesn't win.
jokes.setup: Casual conversation at soccer game
jokes.punchline: Moe's dramatic overstatement about sports outcome
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moe character moment - dramatic, dark, and absurd

jokes.text: It's hard to believe this used to be an internment camp.
jokes.setup: Family enjoying soccer game at stadium
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of stadium's dark historical past
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy juxtaposition delivered matter-of-factly

jokes.text: Yo, paella man, wing one up here!
jokes.setup: Homer wants food at soccer game
jokes.punchline: Confusing paella vendor with hot dog vendor
jokes.explanation: Homer's cultural confusion played for character-appropriate laughs

jokes.text: Hey, look, it's Pelé! Pelé is king of the soccer field. To be king of your kitchen, use Crestfield Wax Paper.
jokes.setup: Excitement about seeing soccer legend Pelé
jokes.punchline: Pelé immediately does commercial endorsement
jokes.explanation: Clever satire of celebrity endorsements with surreal timing

jokes.text: Boring! Come on, you schnorrers! Do something!
jokes.setup: Soccer game progressing slowly
jokes.punchline: Homer's impatience with low-scoring sport
jokes.explanation: Standard American stereotype about soccer but well-executed for Homer

jokes.text: Halfback passes to the center. Back to the wing. Back to the center. Center holds it. Holds it. Holds it.
jokes.setup: Homer describing boring soccer action
jokes.punchline: Repetitive play-by-play emphasizing tedium
jokes.explanation: Well-structured buildup to Homer's breaking point

jokes.text: Oh, that's it. You're dead, pal! Hey, now, that's uncalled for. Shut your hole, Skinner!
jokes.setup: Tension building at boring soccer game
jokes.punchline: Moe's hostile response to Skinner's reasonable mediation
jokes.explanation: Good character work with Skinner as voice of reason and Moe as agitator

jokes.text: You call this a soccer riot? Come on, boys. Let's take 'em to school! Oi! Oi! Oi!
jokes.setup: Fighting breaks out at soccer game
jokes.punchline: Willie's professional assessment of riot quality and European soccer chants
jokes.explanation: Perfect Willie moment - his Scottish background makes him a riot expert

jokes.text: Homer, we've got to get out of here! Oh! But I wanna do some rioting. Jobbers cobknots, ya mucker! All done.
jokes.setup: Marge wants to escape riot
jokes.punchline: Homer's enthusiasm for rioting followed by gibberish and immediate satisfaction
jokes.explanation: Homer's childlike enthusiasm and instant gratification perfectly captured

jokes.text: What began as a traditional soccer riot has escalated into a citywide orgy of destruction. Reacting swiftly, Mayor Quimby has declared mob rule.
jokes.setup: News report on riot aftermath
jokes.punchline: Casual description of 'traditional soccer riot' and official declaration of mob rule
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation and matter-of-fact delivery of increasingly absurd situation

jokes.text: Ex-Ex-Excuse me, sir. I think you've got my TV. Sir?
jokes.setup: Chaos and looting in Springfield
jokes.punchline: Ned's polite approach to retrieving stolen property from dangerous looters
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ned characterization - maintaining politeness in complete chaos

jokes.text: If someone tries to get in here, my burglar alarm will let us know. Watch the fish, Marge.
jokes.setup: Homer claims house is secure with burglar alarm
jokes.punchline: Fish tank immediately crashes, revealing no actual security
jokes.explanation: Classic visual comedy timing - immediate contradiction of Homer's confidence

jokes.text: All the previous owners of this house could still be in here somewhere.
jokes.setup: Salesman explaining home security vulnerabilities
jokes.punchline: Suggesting former owners might be hiding in the house
jokes.explanation: Creepy and absurd fear-mongering sales tactic

jokes.text: I recommend sealing off every door and window with bulletproof Lucite. Wouldn't we all suffocate? Well, I should hope not. Let's get that, the suffocation thing.
jokes.setup: Extreme security recommendation with obvious flaw
jokes.punchline: Salesman's uncertainty about suffocation, Homer wanting the dangerous option
jokes.explanation: Escalating absurdity with perfect Homer logic

jokes.text: $500? Aw, forget it. But surely you can't put a price on your family's lives. I wouldn't have thought so either, but here we are.
jokes.setup: Salesman's emotional appeal about family safety
jokes.punchline: Homer's matter-of-fact acknowledgment that he actually can price his family's lives
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark and perfectly calibrated Homer response - excellent structure and economy

jokes.text: I'd like to buy your deadliest gun, please. Aisle 6, next to the sympathy cards.
jokes.setup: Homer asking for deadliest weapon
jokes.punchline: Casual store location next to sympathy cards
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark juxtaposition - deadly weapons casually placed near grief merchandise

jokes.text: Careful there, Annie Oakley. I don't have to be careful. I got a gun.
jokes.setup: Clerk warning Homer to be careful with gun
jokes.punchline: Homer's backwards logic that having a gun means he doesn't need to be careful
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - completely backwards but delivered with confidence

jokes.text: Loudener.
jokes.setup: After showing silencer
jokes.punchline: Opposite of silencer - makes guns louder
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist invention - the opposite of a practical gun accessory for no reason.

jokes.text: Holster. Oh, yeah. Bandolier. Baby. Silencer. Mm-hmm. Loudener. Oh! Speed-cocker. Ooh! I like the sound of that.
jokes.setup: Clerk listing gun accessories
jokes.punchline: Progression from real to absurd accessories like 'loudener' and 'speed-cocker'
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation from realistic to absurd with perfect pacing

jokes.text: And this is for shooting down police helicopters. Oh, I don't need anything like that... yet.
jokes.setup: Clerk showing extreme military weapon
jokes.punchline: Homer's ominous 'yet' suggesting future police helicopter conflicts
jokes.explanation: Dark humor with perfect timing on 'yet' - implies future escalation

jokes.text: Five days? But I'm mad now! I'd kill you if I had my gun!
jokes.setup: Homer learning about mandatory waiting period
jokes.punchline: Perfectly demonstrating exactly why waiting periods exist
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic structure - Homer proves the policy's necessity while complaining about it

jokes.text: Lousy big shot. Thinks he's so big 'cause he's got a lot of guns. If he didn't have any guns, I'd show him a thing or two.
jokes.setup: Homer angry at gun store clerk
jokes.punchline: Fantasizing about fighting the gun dealer if he didn't have guns to protect himself
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency - Homer's backwards reasoning continues

jokes.text: Dad, it's 3:00 a.m.! Can't you mutter in your room? Marge kicked me out.
jokes.setup: Bart complaining about Homer's late-night muttering
jokes.punchline: Casual revelation that Marge kicked Homer out of bedroom
jokes.explanation: Efficient setup showing marital tension over gun obsession

jokes.text: Oh! How am I supposed to last five days without shooting something?
jokes.setup: Homer's frustration with waiting period
jokes.punchline: Treating shooting things as a basic need
jokes.explanation: Shows Homer's instant obsession and need for immediate gratification

jokes.text: According to your background check, you've been in a mental institution. Yeah. Frequent problems with alcohol. [Chuckling] Yeah. You beat up President Bush. Former president.
jokes.setup: Clerk reading Homer's concerning background check
jokes.punchline: Homer's casual correction about Bush being 'former' president
jokes.explanation: Great callback and escalating background check with Homer's priorities hilariously skewed

jokes.text: Potentially dangerous? Relax. That just limits you to three handguns or less. Whoo-hoo!
jokes.setup: Homer worried about 'potentially dangerous' label
jokes.punchline: Clerk's casual reassurance that he can still buy three handguns
jokes.explanation: Brilliant satire of gun control loopholes - dangerous people still getting multiple weapons

jokes.text: It's a handgun. Isn't it great? This is the trigger, and this is the thing you point at whatever you want to die.
jokes.setup: Homer showing Marge his new gun
jokes.punchline: Casual, childlike description of gun's lethal purpose
jokes.explanation: Dark humor delivered with Homer's innocent enthusiasm

jokes.text: Don't you remember when Maggie shot Mr. Burns? I thought Smithers did it. That would have made a lot more sense.
jokes.setup: Marge referencing previous shooting incident
jokes.punchline: Homer's confusion about who actually shot Mr. Burns
jokes.explanation: Good meta-reference to famous Simpsons mystery with Homer's typical confusion

jokes.text: Dad, can I borrow the gun tomorrow? I wanna scare that old security guard at the bank. Only if you clean your room.
jokes.setup: Bart asking to borrow gun for criminal purposes
jokes.punchline: Homer treating it like a normal parental negotiation about chores
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy - treating armed robbery as normal father-son interaction

jokes.text: If I didn't have this gun, the king of England could just walk in here anytime he wants and start shoving you around. Do you want that? Huh? Do you? No.
jokes.setup: Homer defending Second Amendment rights
jokes.punchline: Completely outdated Revolutionary War logic applied to modern day
jokes.explanation: Perfect satire of gun rights arguments with Homer's historical confusion

jokes.text: A gun is not a weapon, Marge! It's a tool, like a butcher knife or a harpoon or, uh, an alligator.
jokes.setup: Homer defending guns as tools
jokes.punchline: Escalating from reasonable tools to completely absurd comparison with alligator
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation showing Homer's logic completely breaking down

jokes.text: Assault weapons have gotten a lot of bad press lately, but they're manufactured for a reason - to take out today's modern super animals, such as the flying squirrel and the electric eel.
jokes.setup: NRA instructor defending assault weapons
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous justification involving non-threatening 'super animals'
jokes.explanation: Excellent satire of gun lobby justifications with absurd animal threats

jokes.text: Well, it could've been a real ugly situation, but I managed to shoot him in the spine. Yeah, I guess the next place he robs better have a ramp.
jokes.setup: Moe describing stopping a robbery
jokes.punchline: Casual joke about permanently disabling the robber
jokes.explanation: Perfectly dark Moe humor - treating permanent disability as punchline

jokes.text: It seems to me if a gun can protect something as important as a bar, it's good enough to protect my family.
jokes.setup: Homer making case for gun ownership
jokes.punchline: Valuing his bar over his family's importance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer priorities - bar is more important than family in his logic

jokes.text: Don't shoot. Just take the money and get out. What? Oh, Apu, I would never- Or would I?
jokes.setup: Apu assumes Homer is robbing the store
jokes.punchline: Homer considering actually robbing it since he's already assumed to be a robber
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's impressionable nature

jokes.text: I'll rob the Kwik-E-Mart. All right, put your- D'oh! Oh, well, I'll rob it next time.
jokes.setup: Homer deciding to commit robbery
jokes.punchline: Immediately failing and casually postponing criminal activity
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer incompetence with casual attitude toward crime

jokes.text: Can you help me get my ball down from the roof, Dad? Sure thing, honey.
jokes.setup: Lisa asking for normal parental help
jokes.punchline: Homer shooting the ball down with his gun
jokes.explanation: Simple visual gag showing Homer's gun obsession affecting normal activities

jokes.text: See you in hell, dinner plate.
jokes.setup: Homer using gun for household tasks
jokes.punchline: Dramatic declaration of war against dinner plate
jokes.explanation: Absurd drama applied to mundane object - efficient and quotable

jokes.text: Homer, I told you this morning, no guns at the dinner table. You said the breakfast table. It's the same table!
jokes.setup: Marge enforcing no-gun rule
jokes.punchline: Homer's literal interpretation avoiding the spirit of the rule
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - technically correct but completely missing the point

jokes.text: Listen, if it'll make you feel any better, I'll put the safety on. Whoops! Guess it was already on.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to be responsible with gun safety
jokes.punchline: Accidentally firing gun while trying to engage safety
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer incompetence with dangerous situations

jokes.text: No offense, Mom, but that was pretty cool.
jokes.setup: Homer accidentally shooting something at dinner
jokes.punchline: Bart's honest appreciation despite the danger
jokes.explanation: Good Bart character moment - impressed by dangerous things

jokes.text: You said there'd be Fudgeicles, Bart. Where's the Fudgeicles? First, it's Fudgsicle. And I know they're up here. I just need a better foothold.
jokes.setup: Milhouse complaining about missing frozen treats
jokes.punchline: Pedantic correction followed by dangerous climbing setup
jokes.explanation: Good Milhouse character moment with pedantic correction

jokes.text: You said there'd be 'Fudgeicles,' Bart. Where's the 'Fudgeicles'? - First, it's Fudgsicle. And I know they're up here.
jokes.setup: Milhouse looking for ice cream treats
jokes.punchline: Mispronouncing brand name and Bart's correction
jokes.explanation: Mild character humor - Milhouse's mispronunciation and Bart being pedantic.

jokes.text: And the next marksman is William Tell, Jr.! Jinx!
jokes.setup: Homer shooting apple off Bart's head like William Tell
jokes.punchline: Bart's excited 'Jinx!' suggesting this was planned
jokes.explanation: Good historical reference with father-son bonding over dangerous activity

jokes.text: I put it in a safe place, Marge. I mean, what are the odds the boy would look in the vegetable crisper?
jokes.setup: Homer explaining where he hid the gun
jokes.punchline: Assuming vegetable crisper is safe because kids avoid vegetables
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Homer logic - using kids' dislike of vegetables as gun safety

jokes.text: But, Marge, I swear to you, I never thought you'd find out!
jokes.setup: Homer defending his lie about getting rid of gun
jokes.punchline: Honest admission that deception was the plan, not change
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer honesty at the worst possible moment

jokes.text: Do you know how to cook dinner? Do I?
jokes.setup: Homer confident about cooking abilities
jokes.punchline: Visual of him trying to cook with his gun
jokes.explanation: Simple visual gag showing Homer's gun obsession affecting basic tasks

jokes.text: Try the Sleep-Eazy Motel. I woke up there once. Seemed nice.
jokes.setup: Marge asking for motel recommendation
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of mysterious blackout experience
jokes.explanation: Good character humor - implies questionable lifestyle while being helpful.

jokes.text: Hey, this TV's not broken. It's just unplugged. What the-
jokes.setup: Homer discovering TV just needs to be plugged in
jokes.punchline: His confusion cut off by visual gag
jokes.explanation: Simple sight gag setup

jokes.text: I don't see what's wrong with this one. Oh.
jokes.setup: Moe examining 'irregular' Oreo cookies
jokes.punchline: Visual discovery of what makes it irregular
jokes.explanation: Simple visual gag about defective cookies

jokes.text: Looking for a good time, sailor? I certainly am. No, you're not! He's really not.
jokes.setup: Selma propositioning Homer
jokes.punchline: Patty's protective interruption and clarification
jokes.explanation: Good character interaction between the twins and Homer

jokes.text: Homer, you use your gun as a can opener? I use it for everything. Watch me turn on the TV.
jokes.setup: NRA member shocked at Homer's gun misuse
jokes.punchline: Homer proudly demonstrating more misuse
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Homer's complete misunderstanding of gun purpose

jokes.text: Are you some kind of moron? Yeah, but- Hey, yutz. Guns aren't toys.
jokes.setup: NRA member insulting Homer's intelligence
jokes.punchline: Homer's honest self-assessment interrupted
jokes.explanation: Homer's self-awareness cutting through the lecture

jokes.text: They're for family protection, hunting dangerous or delicious animals, and keeping the king of England out of your face.
jokes.setup: NRA member explaining proper gun use
jokes.punchline: Callback to Homer's earlier king of England comment
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Homer's influence on NRA rhetoric

jokes.text: I'd like to order a wake-up call, please. 3:00 a.m. Uh, for every room except this one.
jokes.setup: Bart ordering room service
jokes.punchline: Pranking other hotel guests with 3am wake-up calls
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart prank with good misdirection in setup

jokes.text: Hey, is that a camera in the ceiling? No. Go back to sleep.
jokes.setup: Homer noticing surveillance in sleazy motel
jokes.punchline: Casual denial from hidden observer
jokes.explanation: Creepy motel gag with good economy

jokes.text: Marge? Sorry. Marge? Sorry. Marge? Vote Quimby. Sorry.
jokes.setup: Homer searching hotel rooms for Marge
jokes.punchline: Finding Mayor Quimby in compromising situation, still campaigning
jokes.explanation: Good running gag with Quimby's constant campaigning even in private moments

jokes.text: What's the point of having a gun for protection if you've got no one to protect?
jokes.setup: Homer's moment of realization
jokes.punchline: Finally understanding the circular logic of his situation
jokes.explanation: Good character growth moment with logical realization

jokes.text: Freeze, bad guy! Vote Quimby! Okay, man. Don't shoot. Chill.
jokes.setup: Homer stopping armed robbery
jokes.punchline: Quimby still campaigning during dangerous situation
jokes.explanation: Continuing the Quimby campaign gag in action sequence

jokes.text: I know I said that, but what I secretly meant was- Yoink!
jokes.setup: Homer trying to explain his lie about getting rid of gun
jokes.punchline: Gun gets stolen mid-explanation
jokes.explanation: Good timing with Homer's explanation interrupted by consequences

jokes.text: I felt this incredible surge of power, like God must feel when he's holding a gun.
jokes.setup: Homer describing the appeal of gun ownership
jokes.punchline: Comparing gun ownership to divine power
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy with perfect Homer logic - blasphemous and profound simultaneously
